description: Revise feature 08 (no-zero-value) from completeness+safety to SAFETY-ONLY, per ZERO-SAFETY-MODEL-PLAN.md: an omitted struct/variant field defaults to its zero; only a field whose zero is UNSAFE (nil map/pointer/chan/func, method-bearing interface, or enum/sealed sum) must be set explicitly. `...defaults` stays valid-but-redundant so legacy code keeps compiling. SHARED FACTS: (1) internal/*/*.go are GENERATED from sibling .goal — edit the .goal, run `task generate`, or verify-generated fails. (2) NEVER hand-write *_test.go in emitted dirs; corpus check cases live in testdata/check/<feature>/ with inline `// want` markers; end-to-end tests go in cmd/goal/*_test.go or internal/corpus. (3) The safe/unsafe classifier already exists: `sema.ZeroSafety(typ, decls, info, depth)` returns "" if the zero is SAFE else a reason string (internal/sema/zerosafety.goal). (4) Codes: RETIRE `[missing-field]`; ADD `[unsafe-zero]` (plain omission of an unsafe field); KEEP `[unsafe-default]` UNCHANGED for the `...defaults` path (CheckUnsafeDefaults) — decided to minimize churn. New/removed codes need internal/guide/catalog.goal edits AND `go run ./cmd/goal ai > AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md` regen (gated by cmd/goal TestBootstrapGoldenMatches + TestAICommandEmitsGuide + the catalog-parity test). (5) SPIKE-PROVEN: the US-001 checker change was prototyped and reverted; `task fixpoint` stayed green (the change alters what the checker ACCEPTS, not what code MEANS — generate/build/fixpoint don't run the checker); safe omissions passed, unsafe emitted `[unsafe-zero]`, `...defaults` still emitted `[unsafe-default]`. The exact `task check` fallout was: testdata/check/08-no-zero-value/{incomplete_single,incomplete_struct,variant_incomplete} (TestSemaFieldsRunner + TestASTEngineWholeCorpusBehavioralGate), cmd/goal TestSuggestedFixMissingField, TestBootstrapGoldenMatches, TestAICommandEmitsGuide — nothing else (build-playground was NOT flagged). (6) cmd/goal and cmd/goalc are plain Go. (7) Append the decision to DECISIONS.md.

userStories.title: Make the lexical field check safety-only
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need an omitted SAFE-zero field to be accepted and only an omitted UNSAFE-zero field to error, so the lexical checker enforces safety without completeness ceremony.
acceptanceCriteria: `goalc` on a struct literal omitting only safe-zero fields (string/int/bool/nil-slice/Option) exits 0; omitting a field whose zero is unsafe (nil map/pointer) exits 1 with a located `[unsafe-zero]` error; a `...defaults` literal omitting an unsafe field still emits `[unsafe-default]` (unchanged). A cmd/goal or corpus test asserts all three.
internal/guide/catalog.goal no longer contains a `missing-field` entry and does contain an `unsafe-zero` entry; the catalog-parity test passes and AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md is regenerated.

The three testdata/check/08-no-zero-value/{incomplete_single,incomplete_struct,variant_incomplete}.goal cases are rewritten to omit an UNSAFE field with `// want` markers for `[unsafe-zero]` (they still reject); no `// want [missing-field]` marker remains in testdata.

The missing-field suggested-fix path is resolved (cmd/goal TestSuggestedFixMissingField updated or removed); `grep -rl 'missing-field' internal/ cmd/ testdata/` returns nothing (the code is fully retired from the lexical stage; typed-stage generic/elided-missing-field remain for US-002).
userStories.notes: SPIKE-PROVEN (prototype reverted; redo cleanly). FILES: internal/sema/fields.goal — CheckFields (build `decls := buildDeclKinds(file)` once, thread into both checks); checkStructLit (~64) and checkVariantLit (~168): replace the missing-field emit with a per-field loop mirroring checkUnsafeDefaults (~223): `for _, f := range fields { if present[f.Name] {continue}; reason := ZeroSafety(f.Type, decls, info, 0); if reason == "" {continue}; append Diagnostic{Code:"unsafe-zero", Message: fmt.Sprintf("struct literal `%s{…}` omits field `%s` of type `%s`: %s", name, f.Name, f.Type, reason)} }`. checkStructLit keeps `if litHasSpread(lit) { return nil }` so the `...defaults` path stays CheckUnsafeDefaults' [unsafe-default] (leave CheckUnsafeDefaults UNTOUCHED). Variant message analogous. internal/guide/catalog.goal: remove the `missing-field` line (~59), add `{"unsafe-zero", "08-no-zero-value", "error", "a struct or variant literal omits a field whose zero is unsafe …"}`; keep generic/elided-missing-field (US-002). MIGRATION (spike's exact fallout — all must land in THIS story to keep the gate green): rewrite the 3 incomplete_* testdata cases to omit an unsafe field (e.g. add a `map[string]int` field and omit it) with `// want [unsafe-zero]`; delete/repurpose cmd/goal TestSuggestedFixMissingField (suggestedfix_test.go ~143) since unsafe-zero has no autofix in P1; `go run ./cmd/goal ai > AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md`. `task generate` after .goal edits. Pinned corpus `check` count stays 66 (1:1 conversions — do NOT change generate_test.go). FIXPOINT: unaffected (spike: FIXPOINT OK). PITFALLS: missingFieldNames/missingFieldFix/fieldZeroLit/plural/pronoun/quoteJoin become unused (fine in Go, or delete); the ZeroSafety reason string already ends with actionable guidance — don't append a second 'set it explicitly'; verify docs/by-example.md 'Rejecting an incomplete literal' still rejects (it omits safe fields — if build-playground now fails, rewrite that example to omit an unsafe field here; spike did NOT flag build-playground, so likely fine).

userStories.title: Make the typed depth field check safety-only
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need the typed depth stage's elided/generic literal check to also flag only unsafe-zero omissions, so lexical and typed stages agree on the safety-only rule.
acceptanceCriteria: An elided literal (`[]Inner{{a: 1}}`) or generic literal (`Box[int]{val: 1}`) that omits only safe-zero fields checks clean; one that omits an unsafe-zero field errors; a cmd/goal or corpus test covers both.

internal/guide/catalog.goal no longer contains `generic-missing-field` or `elided-missing-field` (retired in favor of the safety-only rule using `unsafe-zero`, or repurposed); catalog-parity passes and AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md is regenerated.
Any depth-stage 08 tests that asserted the retired completeness codes are updated to the safety-only behavior; the full suite passes.
userStories.notes: DEPENDS ON US-001 (shares the unsafe-zero code + semantics). FILES: internal/typecheck/nozero.goal — CheckNoZeroValue / litDiag (generic-missing-field ~113, elided-missing-field ~117): instead of flagging any omitted field, resolve each omitted field's go/types.Type and flag only if its zero is unsafe. Mirror sema.ZeroSafety's rules over go/types: *types.Pointer / *types.Map / *types.Chan / *types.Signature -> unsafe; *types.Interface with methods -> unsafe; a named type whose underlying resolves from a goal enum/sealed sum -> unsafe (detect via the marker method or the sema Info if threaded); *types.Slice / *types.Array / *types.Basic / bare interface{} / error -> safe. Emit code `unsafe-zero` (consistent with US-001) at the .goal-mapped position. catalog.goal: remove generic-missing-field + elided-missing-field. Re-baseline typed-stage 08 tests (cmd/goal/gotypeerror_test.go, main_test.go, internal/corpus) that expected those codes. PITFALLS: the go/types safety classifier MUST match sema.ZeroSafety exactly or the two stages disagree (a case clean at one stage, error at the other); confirm the enum/sealed detection over lowered Go (the §8.1 encoding's marker method) matches what sema treats as a sum type; regen bootstrap.

userStories.title: Revise spec and docs for safety-only
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the spec, DECISIONS ledger, and STATUS doc to describe the safety-only model, so the guarantee's definition matches the checker.
acceptanceCriteria: goal-design-spec.md §3.5 describes feature 08 as safety-only (omitted fields default; only unsafe zeros must be named) and notes `...defaults` is now redundant-but-accepted; `grep -q 'unsafe-zero' goal-design-spec.md` and no stale 'must name every field' completeness claim remains for §3.5.

DECISIONS.md gains a dated §08-revision entry recording the choice (drop completeness, keep `...defaults` for compatibility, `[missing-field]` -> `[unsafe-zero]`) and the rejected alternatives (global bare-zero, `struct zero` opt-in) with why.
docs/STATUS.md's feature-08 line reflects safety-only; `grep -q 'safety-only' docs/STATUS.md` succeeds.
userStories.notes: DEPENDS ON US-001/002 (docs describe the final behavior). Docs are not toolchain-verified (unlike docs/by-example.md, which build-playground checks), so these edits are low-risk for the gate. FILES: goal-design-spec.md §3.5; DECISIONS.md (append, do not rewrite history); docs/STATUS.md (the §(a)/(c) feature-08 rows). If docs/by-example.md's 'Rejecting an incomplete literal' example was NOT already rewritten in US-001, rewrite it here to omit an unsafe field and regen AI-KNOWLEDGE-BOOTSTRAP.md + features.json (that example IS build-playground-verified, so it must still reject). Keep prose consistent with the retired [missing-field] / new [unsafe-zero] naming.
